themes.exampleQuote: I've been to Belgium several times and this place has waffles that are just as good as what you could get in liège.
I'm an ice cream snob and this is some of the best ice cream I've ever had.
The owner is extremely friendly and nice and helped make the experience that much better.
The comment along the lines of 'I'm not going to waste any more ice cream on you all,' delivered in a rude tone, made us feel uncomfortable.
The sage salted almond on a waffle was so good.

notable_quote: Add that delicious waffle to it and you go into a brand new realm of delicious.
sentiment_summary: Reviewers consistently reach for superlatives on both the ice cream and the Liège waffles, with specific flavors like sage salted almond earning their own callouts, but a recurring undercurrent around sample-tasting interactions has drawn two pointed critical reviews that both describe the same staff dynamic.
quarter_narrative: Treat enters Spring 2026 with 623 Google reviews sitting at 98% five stars and zero Instagram posts in the last 199 days — a business that Pittsburgh clearly loves and is largely documenting for itself. No Pittsburgh TikTok videos are indexed for the shop either, which means the city's appetite for exactly this kind of photogenic, concept-forward dessert experience is not yet finding Treat as its subject. That contrast matters in a family where Page's is running away with the conversation on the strength of social momentum alone. For Treat to move off the Ones to Watch tier, the rank math is straightforward: the product signal and the review signal are already there; the missing variable is any evidence that the city is talking about it somewhere other than Google.
tldr_read: Near-perfect reviews and a fully built conversion path, but the Instagram has been dark for 199 days and no TikTok presence exists — Ones to Watch, #3 in Pittsburgh Ice Cream Shops.
tldr_meaning: Every signal axis is at or above the family median except Instagram momentum, which is effectively flatlined, and that single gap is the difference between this rank and the top of the family.

playbook.headline: End the 199-day Instagram silence
playbook.action: One post per week featuring named flavors — sage salted almond has a ready-made audience and zero current documentation.

playbook.headline: Make the sample policy visible before it's verbal
playbook.action: A simple posted limit prevents the recurring negative interaction that has now appeared in two separate reviews and drawn a public owner response.

playbook.headline: Give the 7.65% engagement rate somewhere to land
playbook.action: With an engagement rate nearly double the family median, any resumed posting will find an audience that is already listening — link the menu and a booking or order path in the bio.
